summary: Comprehensive backend (pytest, 11/11 passing) and frontend (Playwright, 25/25 assertions passing) testing of Muk Buddy landing page. All requested features verified: lead form CRUD with persistence, validation, honeypot, rate limit, security headers, Vimeo embed, CTAs, all 15 sections, FAQ accordion, mobile responsiveness.
critical_code_review_comments: /docs, /redoc, /openapi.json — backend disables them via FastAPI (docs_url=None) but the K8s ingress routes non-/api paths to the React SPA, which returns index.html with status 200. Effectively docs are NOT exposed (verified no Swagger/ReDoc/OpenAPI content in response), so the security goal is met — but if a strict 404 is required, an explicit FastAPI route returning 404 for these paths would be cleaner.

Rate limiter is in-memory per-process (collections.deque). Will not work correctly behind multiple workers/replicas; for production scale move to Redis-backed limiter (e.g., slowapi w/ redis backend).
_client_ip trusts X-Forwarded-For without ingress validation — fine for current single-ingress setup; document assumption.
Lead 'meta.user_agent' truncation at 300 chars is good; consider also stripping ip from logs for PII compliance.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed leads in mongo and clean them up via pymongo after each test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests at /app/backend/tests/test_mukbuddy_api.py; rerun with: pytest backend/tests/test_mukbuddy_api.py -v. Rate-limit test uses unique X-Forwarded-For per test to avoid cross-test 429s. Frontend live URL: https://two-chamber-test.preview.emergentagent.com. All key elements have data-testid attributes (hero-*, header-*, lead-*, faq-item-N, *-section).
